• 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Linda D. Hamilton 
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk 
Superior Court/County Clerk 
County of San Bernardino 
July 19, 1988 
351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor 
San Bernardino, 
CA 
92415-0210 
Dear Ms. Hamilton: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-251 
I am responding to your letter of June 27, 1988. 
You have 
requested clarification of a previous advice letter (Hamilton 
Advice Letter, No. A-88-141) regarding the officer with whom 
copies of candidate statements of economic interests (Form 721) 
are filed urider the Political Reform Act.lI 
QUESTION 
Which county official is responsible for retaining copies of 
statements of economic interests filed by nonjudicial candidates 
for county office? 
CONCLUSION 
Copies of statements of economic interests filed by candidates 
for nonjudicial county office are retained by the county clerk. 
ANALYSIS 
Candidates for specific elected offices are required to file 
statements of economic interests. 
(Sections 87200 and 87201.) 
The filing official who receives candidates' statements is 
lIGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references' are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Linda D. Hamilton 
2 
identified by the statute for each type of otfice sought. 
(Section 87500.) 
For instance, candidates for the offices of 
state legislator and state Board of Equalization are required to 
file statements of economic interests with the officer who 
receives their declarations of candidacy. 
(Section 87500(d).) 
Candidates for county boards of supervisors are required to file 
statements of economic interests with the clerk of the county in 
which they are elected. 
(Section 87500(f).) 
The only exception to precise identification of the filing 
official is for candidates for judicial office. 
(Section 
87500(i).) 
Because the statute contains no reference to 
candidates for judicial office, the previous advice letter to you 
(Hamilton, supra) recommended that copies of statements of 
economic interests filed by'nonincumbent candidates for judicial 
office should be retained by the clerk of the court. 
That advice 
is pending statutory change which may. remove any ambiguity 
regarding who is the proper filing official. 
• 
In your most recent letter, dated June 27, 1988, you ask for 
clarification as to the proper filing official for nonjudicial 
candidates. 
A filing official retains copies of candidate 
statements of economic interests.~ The summary shown below 
identifies the filing official for each category of candidate 
filer which you listed in the enclosure with your letter. 
FILING OFFICIALS FOR COUNTY 721 FILERS 
Where to File 
Nonincumbent 
Category of filer 
Code section 
Incumbents 
candidates only 
Superior court 
87500 (i) 
clerk of court 
clerk of court 
judges and court 
(see FPPC Advice 
commissioners 
letter No. A-88-
and candidates 
141) 
Chief administrative 
87500(e) 
county clerk 
county clerk 
officers 
DAs and candidates 
county counsel and 
candidates 
Supervisors and 
candidates 
County planning 
87500(g) 
own agency 
not applicable 
commissioners 
~A "filing official" makes and retains a copy of a disclosure 
statement but does not retain the original statement. 
originals are 
forwarded to the FPPC, which is the filing officer. 


• 
Linda D. Hamilton 
Page 3 
If you have further questions, please call me at (916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

Political Reform Consultant 


SUPERIOR COURT/COUNTY CLERK 
COUNTY OF SAN BERNAROINO 
IX! 351 No. Arrowhead Ave .• Fifth Floor. San Bernardino. CA 92415-0210 • 17141381-3921 
o West District. 8303 Heven Avenue. Rancho Cucamonga. CA 91130 • 17141945-4136 
o Desert District. 14455 Civic Drive. VictorvlUe. CA 92392 • 16191243-8610 
( 
o North o..ert District. 235 E. Mountain View Avenue. Ber,tow. CA 92311 • 16191 256-4816 
• 
June 27, 1988 
Ms. Lilly Spitz 
Counsel, Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commi~sion 
PO Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Re: 
Request for Advice 
Your File No. A-88-l4l 
Dear Ms. Spitz: 
" 
We need further clarification on your response to the above 
request. 
Please refer to the attached copy of my April 6th 
letter. 
I was and am now again requesting clarification as 
to the filing of all candidates' statements. 
The Registrar 
of Voters forward~to us the candidate statements for all 
elected offices (see attached list and memo). 
As you know, the declarations of candidacy are filed with 
the Registrar of Voters and the Form 72l's as indicated in 
my April 6th letter are filed with this office. 
But, we 
desire verification as to whether the candidate statements, 
Forms 721, (see attached Final Candidate List for 1988 
filings) should be filed with us, the Registrar of Voters, 
or some other department. 
We are requesting a letter of intrepretation as to the 
above. 
If you require further information, please contact me at 
(7l4) 387-3970. 
Sincerely, 
Linda D. Hamilton 
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk 


/ 
/ 
.S.U.P.E.R.'O •.• R.C.O.U.R.T.IC.O.U.N.T.Y.C.L.E.R.K_._._ !'~ 
Gf. 361 No. AlTOwhead Ave .. Fifth Floor. San Bernardino. CA 92415·0210 • 17141 387·3921

...... 
o We.t Dlltrict. 8303 Hlven Avenue. Rancho Cucamonga. CA 91730 • (714' 945·4136 
/rf4/11\\\~' 
COUNTY OF SAN BERNARDINI 
:::: De .. rt District. 14455 Civic Drive. Victorville. CA 92392 • (619,243·8670 
C North De.ert District. 235 E. Mountain View Avenue. Barstow. CA 92311 • (619' 256·4816 
• 
April 6, 1988 
Mr. Bruce Robeck 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Mr. Robeck: 
Pursuant to our telephone conversation of April 1, 1988, 
we are requesting a Letter of Interpretation for guidelines 
as to where Candidate Statements' Forms 721's are to be 
filed in San Bernardino County. 
The Registrar receives all other campaign/election documents 
but feels that the Forms 721's are to be filed with the 
County Clerk's Office. 
Enclosed is a copy of a transmittal 
memo from the Registrar of Voters to the Technical 
Assistance and Analysis Division on this subject . 
The County Clerk's Office receives Forms 721 for the 
Superior Court Judges, Commissioners, CAO, County Counsel, 
District Attorney, Board of Supervisors and Planning 
Commissioners. 
After a review of our records for the past 3 
years, we found no record of Candidate Statements being 
received or filed in our office. 
Therefore, a Letter of Interpretation is requested at this 
time. 
If you require further information, please contact me at 
(714) 387-3970. 
Sincerely, 
Linda D. Hamilton 
Secretary/Deputy County Clerk 
cc:Audrey Butler, Deputy Registrar

Confirming my telephone call with your office on March 14, and your 
reply that information would be forthcoming in about a week with your 
Memo on the handling of Form 72ls. 
Your memo was received on 
March 21 and we are forwarding 34 copies of Candidates' Form 721. 
Your information that documents be date stamped was received after 
the fact; also, inasmuch as the County Clerk is the filing officer, 
we are also sending a copy there to be filed. 
Please call A. Butler, 714/387-2082, if you have any questions or 
if we can be of further service.

sections.question:
Which county official is responsible for retaining copies of 
statements of economic interests filed by nonjudicial candidates 
for county office?
sections.conclusion:
Copies of statements of economic interests filed by candidates 
for nonjudicial county office are retained by the county clerk.
sections.analysis:
Candidates for specific elected offices are required to file 
statements of economic interests. 
(Sections 87200 and 87201.) 
The filing official who receives candidates' statements is 
l, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Linda D. Hamilton 
2 
identified by the statute for each type of otfice sought. 
(Section 87500.) 
For instance, candidates for the offices of 
state legislator and state Board of Equalization are required to 
file statements of economic interests with the officer who 
receives their declarations of candidacy. 
(Section 87500(d).) 
Candidates for county boards of supervisors are required to file 
statements of economic interests with the clerk of the county in 
which they are elected. 
(Section 87500(f).) 
The only exception to precise identification of the filing 
official is for candidates for judicial office. 
(Section 
87500(i).) 
Because the statute contains no reference to 
candidates for judicial office, the previous advice letter to you 
(Hamilton, supra) recommended that copies of statements of 
economic interests filed by'nonincumbent candidates for judicial 
office should be retained by the clerk of the court. 
That advice 
is pending statutory change which may. remove any ambiguity 
regarding who is the proper filing official. 
• 
In your most recent letter, dated June 27, 1988, you ask for 
clarification as to the proper filing official for nonjudicial 
candidates. 
A filing official retains copies of candidate 
statements of economic interests.~ The summary shown below 
identifies the filing official for each category of candidate 
filer which you listed in the enclosure with your letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Which county official is responsible for retaining copies of 
statements of economic interests filed by nonjudicial candidates 
for county office?

CONCLUSION: Copies of statements of economic interests filed by candidates 
for nonjudicial county office are retained by the county clerk.
embedding.first_500_words: • California Fair Political Practices Commission Linda D. Hamilton Secretary/Deputy County Clerk Superior Court/County Clerk County of San Bernardino July 19, 1988 351 No. Arrowhead Ave., Fifth Floor San Bernardino, CA 92415-0210 Dear Ms. Hamilton: Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-88-251 I am responding to your letter of June 27, 1988. You have requested clarification of a previous advice letter (Hamilton Advice Letter, No. A-88-141) regarding the officer with whom copies of candidate statements of economic interests (Form 721) are filed urider the Political Reform Act.lI QUESTION Which county official is responsible for retaining copies of statements of economic interests filed by nonjudicial candidates for county office? CONCLUSION Copies of statements of economic interests filed by candidates for nonjudicial county office are retained by the county clerk. ANALYSIS Candidates for specific elected offices are required to file statements of economic interests. (Sections 87200 and 87201.) The filing official who receives candidates' statements is lIGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references' are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 Linda D. Hamilton 2 identified by the statute for each type of otfice sought. (Section 87500.) For instance, candidates for the offices of state legislator and state Board of Equalization are required to file statements of economic interests with the officer who receives their declarations of candidacy. (Section 87500(d).) Candidates for county boards of supervisors are required to file statements of economic interests with the clerk of the county in which they are elected. (Section 87500(f).) The only exception to precise identification of the filing official is for candidates for judicial office. (Section 87500(i).) Because the statute contains no reference to candidates for judicial office, the previous advice letter to you (Hamilton, supra) recommended that copies of statements of economic interests filed by'nonincumbent candidates for judicial office should be retained by the clerk of the court. That advice is pending statutory change which may. remove any ambiguity regarding who is the proper filing official. • In your most recent letter, dated June 27, 1988, you ask for clarification as to the proper filing official for nonjudicial candidates. A filing official retains copies of candidate statements of economic interests.~ The summary shown below identifies the filing official for each category of candidate filer which you listed in the enclosure with your letter. FILING OFFICIALS FOR COUNTY 721 FILERS Where to File Nonincumbent Category of filer Code section Incumbents candidates only Superior court 87500 (i) clerk of court clerk of court judges and court (see FPPC Advice commissioners letter No. A-88- and candidates 141) Chief administrative 87500(e) county clerk county clerk officers DAs and candidates county counsel and candidates Supervisors and candidates County planning 87500(g) own agency not applicable commissioners ~A "filing official" makes and retains
